context: hev ask is an Astro 5 integration that adds a ⌘K command-palette search overlay to documentation sites whose content lives in Astro content collections. It offers two search paths from one endpoint: an instant, keyless keyword path that runs as the reader types, and an optional Claude-powered agentic path triggered by pressing Enter that streams a grounded answer with inline deep links. Both paths chunk documents by heading so links land on the exact section anchor rather than the top of a page. The agentic path reads from an offline-built, git-committed knowledge graph (kg.json) — a distilled "shadow site" of section nodes — built by the hev-ask-kg CLI and inlined into the build via a virtual module. The system is designed to degrade gracefully (no key, stale or missing graph) and to never hard-fail. This corpus documents the concepts, quick start, tradeoffs, limits, and the full API surface (integration config, SearchOverlay component, /api/ask endpoint, knowledge-graph format, and the CLI).

glossary.definition: An Astro integration that adds a ⌘K search overlay with instant keyword search and an optional Claude-powered agentic search path.
An offline-built, git-committed JSON artifact of distilled section nodes (with verbatim facts, summaries, and source links) that the agentic loop reasons from.
A bounded Claude tool-use loop, triggered by Enter, that opens sections it needs and then streams a grounded answer with inline deep links.
The dependency-free, keyless path that scores chunks by token overlap (widened by the glossary) and returns ranked section deep links as the reader types.
A document slice split on a heading, carrying the heading, cleaned prose, and a basePath+slug+#anchor URL; the unit hev ask indexes.
The #id appended to a section URL, generated with github-slugger so it matches Astro's rendered heading id.
The single binary shipping with @hev/ask, offering build and verify subcommands to generate and check the committed knowledge graph.
The default export of @hev/ask: the Astro integration configured with an options object in astro.config.mjs.
The Astro component that renders the ⌘K command palette and posts queries to the search endpoint.
The on-demand route the integration injects; returns keyword JSON or an agentic SSE stream depending on mode and key presence.
The server-side secret that enables the agentic path and the offline graph build; never exposed to the browser.
A sha256 of concatenated chunk text used to decide whether the graph needs regeneration; build skips the model call when a committed graph matches.
The same slug library Astro's renderer uses, adopted as hev ask's single dependency to keep generated anchors byte-identical to rendered heading ids.
A node mode flag: source-primary nodes (reference/API sections) are read verbatim by the agent, while agent-primary nodes are read as paraphrased summaries.
Terms with aliases and definitions in the knowledge graph that widen keyword queries (e.g. k8s → kubernetes) before retrieval.
The cap on how many search tool rounds the agentic loop runs before it must stop and answer (default 4).
A Node/Cloudflare/Vercel adapter required because the search endpoint renders on demand; static-only builds cannot serve search.
The CLI subcommand that builds the site and checks anchors, coverage, and literal fidelity against the committed graph without calling the model.

nodes.title: CLI: hev-ask-kg
nodes.summary: The hev-ask-kg command: build the committed knowledge graph offline (hash-gated) and verify every heading anchor resolves in the built HTML, with all flags. @hev/ask ships one binary, hev-ask-kg, with two commands: bu…

facts.literal:
pnpm exec hev-ask-kg build      # one-shot build (needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
pnpm exec hev-ask-kg corpus     # emit the sections to distil (keyless)
pnpm exec hev-ask-kg assemble   # assemble kg.json from a distillation (keyless)
pnpm exec hev-ask-kg verify     # verify anchors, coverage, fidelity

nodes.title: CLI: hev-ask-kg
nodes.heading: build
nodes.summary: build Builds the knowledge graph and writes it to kgPath (default .hev-ask/kg.json). It reads your content from disk, chunks it, and hashes the result. If a committed kg.json already matches that hash, it exits without…

facts.literal:
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
pnpm exec hev-ask-kg build
# → [hev-ask] kg:written .hev-ask/kg.json (42 chunks)
#   or kg:skipped when the hash already matches

hev-ask-kg build --collection docs --collection guides --chunk-heading-depth 2
hev-ask-kg verify --skip-build

nodes.title: CLI: hev-ask-kg
nodes.heading: verify
nodes.summary: verify Builds the site, then checks the committed graph against the corpus. It reads only committed files — it never calls the model — so it is a clean CI gate. Three checks: Anchors (always fatal). Every chunk's #anchor…

facts.literal:
pnpm exec hev-ask-kg verify
# → [hev-ask] verified 128 anchors
[hev-ask] missing anchor old-name for /docs/concepts#old-name in dist/docs/concepts/index.html

nodes.title: SearchOverlay component
nodes.heading: The mode toggle
nodes.summary: The mode toggle The overlay persists an "AI on Enter" preference in localStorage under the key hev-ask:mode (agentic or keyword). Readers who never want the model call can flip it off; the choice survives reloads.

nodes.title: Concepts
nodes.heading: Asking is the default
nodes.summary: Asking is the default The overlay is ask-first. A single word is treated as a keyword lookup and answered instantly from the index. The moment the query grows past one word — the reader types a space — the overlay stops…

nodes.summary: Chunks and anchors hev ask does not index pages — it indexes sections. Each document is split on its headings (up to a configurable depth, default ## and ###). Content before the first heading becomes the intro chunk wh…

nodes.heading: Agentic search adds latency
nodes.summary: Agentic search adds latency The agentic path is bounded by maxIterations (default 4) Claude round-trips — worst case a few seconds. It is not instant, by nature. The keyword path is the instant lane and always available;…

nodes.summary: Anchors depend on Astro's slugger Deep links are only correct as long as the heading slugs hev ask generates match Astro's rendered id attributes. hev ask uses the same github-slugger to stay aligned, and ships a hev-f…

nodes.summary: Frontmatter parsing is a flat-YAML subset The offline build parses frontmatter with a small flat-YAML splitter, not a full YAML parser. It handles the common docs schema (string and number fields). Nested frontmatter str…

nodes.summary: Recall has a keyword ceiling Retrieval is keyword token-overlap widened by the glossary — not embeddings. The agentic loop grounds its answer in what retrieval surfaces; it cannot ground in — or link to — what retrieval…

nodes.summary: The corpus is your content collection hev ask searches the Astro content collection(s) you configure, and nothing else. There is no crawler, no external sitemap ingestion, and no way to index pages that aren't collectio…

facts.literal:
---
// src/layouts/Base.astro
import SearchOverlay from "@hev/ask/components/SearchOverlay.astro";
---
<button type="button" data-hev-ask-open>
  Search <kbd>⌘K</kbd>
</button>

<slot />

<SearchOverlay />

nodes.title: Quick start
nodes.heading: 5. Build the knowledge graph
nodes.summary: 5. Build the knowledge graph The knowledge graph is an offline-built JSON file committed to your repo. It gives the agentic loop domain context and a glossary (so k8s finds kubernetes). Build it once, then whenever conte…

facts.literal:
You: build the hev ask knowledge graph

Claude runs:
  hev-ask-kg corpus            # emits the sections to distil
  …writes context/glossary/summaries/suggestions…
  hev-ask-kg assemble          # writes .hev-ask/kg.json (deterministically)

export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
pnpm exec hev-ask-kg build      # writes .hev-ask/kg.json

pnpm exec hev-ask-kg verify     # builds the site, checks every anchor resolves
git add .hev-ask/kg.json
